title: Michael B. Jordan Wins Best Actor at 2026 Oscars - Deadline
text: Michael B. Jordan claimed his first Academy Award in the Best Actor category for his dual roles in Warner Bros. Sinners at the 2026 Oscars ceremony. Key Moments from His Speech: Receiving a standing ovation at the Dolby Theatre, Jordan opened by saying God is good and acknowledged his family members present, including his mother and father who had flown in from Ghana. He specifically praised the studio leadership, thanking them for betting on the culture, on original ideas and original artistry. Jordan also expressed deep gratitude to director Ryan Coogler, calling him an amazing person and a valued collaborator. He recognized his castmates, particularly Wunmi Mosaku and Hailee Steinfeld, who was noted to be at home preparing for childbirth. He also paid homage to pioneering Black actors including Sidney Poitier, Denzel Washington, and Will Smith, expressing honor at standing among such figures. Sinners, set in 1930s Mississippi Delta, follows twin brothers returning home and defending their community against supernatural threats. The film garnered a record 16 Oscar nominations and won four awards, including Original Screenplay and Cinematography.
